docs.sui.io Analytics

2026-04-20 to 2026-04-26 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260427T155118Z

Summary Traffic Top Pages Referrals Top Referrer URLs Page↔Theme Takeaways Topics User Engagement Sprint
Overview

Executive Summary

Kapa Questions
694
Themes Identified
10
Visitors (7d)
4,388
Roughly half the 14-day total (8,512), suggesting a relatively stable week-over-week baseline with no major spike or drop.
Pageviews (7d)
12,664
Averages ~1,809 pageviews/day; the 7d total is about 50% of the 30d total (56,388), indicating consistent daily traffic.
Bounce Rate
64%
Consistent across 7d, 14d, and 30d (all 64–65%), signaling a persistent pattern of single-page visits, likely driven by users landing on specific reference pages.
Views Per Visit
2.62
Slightly lower than the 14d (2.67) and 30d (2.54) averages, suggesting typical shallow browsing depth across the doc site.
Avg. Visit Duration
154 seconds
Below the 14d (158s) and 30d (160s) averages, hinting that this week's visitors engaged slightly less deeply than the broader trend.
This week, docs.sui.io traffic was dominated by onboarding and data-access migration content, reflecting the July 2026 JSON-RPC deprecation deadline driving urgent developer action. The chatbot handled broad topic coverage but struggled most with Ecosystem/Onboarding and Data Access Migration questions, signaling gaps in documentation discoverability and API parity clarity.
Plausible

Traffic Overview

Over the 7-day period April 20–26, 2026, docs.sui.io attracted 4,388 visitors generating 12,664 pageviews, with a 64% bounce rate, 2.62 views per visit, and an average session duration of 154 seconds. Traffic is heavily weighted toward weekdays, with a notable weekend dip (491–485 visitors on Apr 25–26 vs. 708–751 midweek). The onboarding funnel, API references, and DeepBook docs dominate page engagement. Direct traffic and Google are the top acquisition channels, with ChatGPT and Perplexity appearing as emerging AI referral sources.

Weekday vs. Weekend Traffic Drop

Visitors fell sharply from 751 on Wednesday Apr 22 to 491 on Friday Apr 25 and 485 on Saturday Apr 26, a ~35% decline. Pageviews dropped even more steeply (2,189 → 908 → 1,182), confirming docs.sui.io is primarily used by professional/weekday developers.

AI Referral Traffic Emerging

ChatGPT (37 visitors), Perplexity (4), and Zalo chat (6, likely AI-assisted) collectively sent ~47 visitors this week. While still small (<1% of total), this represents a growing pattern of AI assistants routing users to official docs — a trend likely to accelerate.

gRPC Documentation Deep Engagement

The /develop/accessing-data/grpc/using-grpc page recorded the highest time-on-page at 217 seconds, and the parent /develop/accessing-data/grpc page also ranked high. This signals growing developer interest in gRPC as a data access method, possibly tied to new infrastructure tooling.

Onboarding Funnel Dominates Traffic

The top 8 pages include 5 onboarding-related pages (/getting-started/onboarding, sui-install, hello-world, get-coins, configure-sui-client), collectively accounting for a large share of pageviews. Low bounce rates on hub pages (10–19%) confirm the funnel is working well.

DeepBook Ecosystem Driving Targeted Traffic

deepbook.tech sent 116 visitors (3rd largest non-direct source) directly to DeepBook docs, and /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook is a top-5 page by pageviews. This cross-site referral pattern indicates an active DeFi developer community around DeepBook.

Plausible

Top 20 Pages

# Page Insight
1 / Top page with 1,580 pageviews and a low 37% bounce rate, indicating most homepage visitors explore further into the docs.
2 /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install 479 visitors and 712 pageviews with a 56% bounce rate; the primary entry point for new developers setting up Sui.
3 /getting-started/onboarding/hello-world 213 visitors, 70% bounce rate — many users may complete the install step and leave after the hello-world tutorial.
4 /sui-api-ref 151 visitors but only 275 pageviews with a low 33% bounce rate, suggesting API reference users browse multiple endpoints per session.
5 /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook 183 visitors with a low 36% bounce rate; strong engagement from DeFi/DeepBook developers, corroborated by deepbook.tech referrals.
6 /getting-started/onboarding/get-coins 182 visitors; a key step in the onboarding flow, frequently visited alongside install and hello-world pages.
7 /getting-started/onboarding 192 visitors with a very low 10% bounce rate — serves as an effective hub that funnels users deeper into onboarding steps.
8 /references/fullnode-protocol 90 visitors generating 227 pageviews (2.5x ratio), indicating node operators return or browse multiple protocol reference pages.
9 /getting-started 151 visitors with only 19% bounce rate; strong gateway page that successfully routes users to sub-sections.
10 /onchain-finance/tokenized-assets/create-nft 210 visitors with a low 14% bounce rate; NFT creation is a popular and sticky onboarding topic.
11 /getting-started/tooling 154 visitors with a 31% bounce rate; developers frequently continue from tooling docs to adjacent references.
12 /getting-started/onboarding/configure-sui-client 112 visitors, 16% bounce rate — highly engaged users in the middle of the onboarding sequence.
13 /develop/accessing-data/grpc 107 visitors, 42% bounce rate, 76s scroll depth — gRPC data access is a notable area of developer interest.
14 /references/cli 80 visitors with a very low 15% bounce rate; CLI reference users tend to explore multiple commands per session.
15 /onchain-finance/fungible-tokens/token-vesting-strategies 112 visitors with only 10% bounce rate — highly engaging content; users explore the full vesting strategy section.
16 /develop/accessing-data/graphql/graphql-rpc 80 visitors; GraphQL alongside gRPC signals strong interest in data access patterns for developers.
17 /references/fullnode-protocol-types Only 25 visitors but 119 pageviews and 82% bounce rate — likely a deep-reference page reached via search with high exit after finding the answer.
18 /develop/accessing-data/grpc/using-grpc 58 visitors but 217s time-on-page — the longest engagement of any page, indicating developers carefully read through gRPC usage instructions.
19 /develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview 84 visitors with an unusually high 124% bounce rate value (possible data anomaly) and 42s scroll depth; tokenomics attracts curious but less committed readers.
20 /operators/full-node/sui-full-node 94 visitors with 35% bounce rate; steady operator audience setting up full nodes, consistent with fullnode-protocol referral traffic.
Plausible

Referral Sources

Source Type Insight
Direct / None Organic 1,998 visitors (45% of total), the largest single source. Low bounce (71%) relative to some search channels, with 112s avg duration — likely returning developers and bookmarked links.
Google Organic 1,091 visitors, 54% bounce rate, and the highest avg duration at 251s — organic search visitors are highly engaged and likely finding targeted answers.
sui.io Organic 485 visitors referred from the main Sui website, 49% bounce, 209s duration — warm traffic from the ecosystem homepage with strong intent.
Bing Organic 421 visitors with a very high 88% bounce rate and only 59s duration — Bing traffic is largely shallow, likely finding quick answers and leaving.
deepbook.tech Organic 116 visitors, 49% bounce, 120s duration — a dedicated DeFi protocol site driving relevant, engaged developer traffic to DeepBook docs.
GitHub Organic 53 visitors, 51% bounce, 111s — developers following links from repos; moderate engagement suggesting code-context browsing.
docs.sui.io Organic 48 visitors self-referred (internal links tracked as referral), 45% bounce, and notably high 435s duration — deep internal navigation sessions.
blog.sui.io Organic 38 visitors, 89% bounce, only 13s duration — blog readers click through but quickly leave; content alignment between blog and docs could improve.
chatgpt.com AI / Chatbot 37 visitors referred from ChatGPT, 74% bounce, 26s duration — AI assistant users arriving with specific questions but not deeply exploring docs.
Twitter Organic 30 visitors, 53% bounce, 132s duration — social media-driven traffic is moderate but reasonably engaged.
Baidu Organic 27 visitors, 89% bounce, ~0s duration — very shallow Chinese search engine traffic, nearly all single-page exits.
suiplay.sui.io Organic 24 visitors from the SuiPlay gaming platform; signals crossover between gaming ecosystem and developer documentation needs.
DuckDuckGo Organic 20 visitors, 65% bounce, 40s duration — privacy-focused search users with moderate engagement.
evefrontier.com Organic 8 visitors, 50% bounce, 52s — a gaming project built on Sui driving small but targeted developer traffic.
coinglass.com Organic 8 visitors, 100% bounce rate — crypto analytics users arrive but immediately leave; content may not match their expectations.
quicknode.com Organic 7 visitors, 67% bounce, 378s duration — despite low volume, QuickNode-referred users spend significant time, suggesting infrastructure developers with deep research needs.
chat.zalo.me AI / Chatbot 6 visitors from Zalo chat (Vietnamese messaging app), 67% bounce, 462s duration — very small but surprisingly high time-on-page; likely shared links in developer communities.
Perplexity AI / Chatbot 4 visitors, 100% bounce — AI search engine sending a small trickle of traffic; all single-page exits suggest users got their answer immediately.
Coccoc Organic 4 visitors from Coccoc (Vietnamese browser/search), 50% bounce, 479s duration — tiny but highly engaged Southeast Asian developer segment.
hub.despread.io Organic 4 visitors from a crypto research/analytics hub with 50% bounce and 44s duration — niche but relevant ecosystem traffic.
AI Referral Detail

chatgpt.com

37 visitors with 74% bounce and only 26s average duration — ChatGPT users arrive with a specific question answered by the AI and leave immediately without deeper exploration.

Perplexity

4 visitors, all single-page exits with 100% bounce — Perplexity answers queries completely, sending only residual traffic that does not engage further.

chat.zalo.me

6 visitors from Zalo chat with 67% bounce but a surprisingly high 462s average duration — likely developer community link shares in Vietnamese-language Sui groups leading to deep reading sessions.

Cross-Signal

Page ↔ Kapa Theme Correlations

High-traffic pages mapped to the Kapa themes they correspond to.

Page Related Kapa Theme Insight
/develop/accessing-data/grpc/using-grpc Data Access Migration (JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL) Highest time-on-page (217s) confirms developers carefully study gRPC usage amid migration urgency.
/develop/accessing-data/grpc Data Access Migration (JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL) 107 visitors with 42% bounce signals gRPC interest but partial satisfaction from current docs.
/develop/accessing-data/graphql/graphql-rpc Data Access Migration (JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL) 80 visitors alongside gRPC page confirms developers evaluating both migration paths simultaneously.
/getting-started/onboarding/sui-install Package Deployment & Publishing 479 visitors and repeated 'How do I deploy' bot questions indicate onboarding friction persists.
/onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook Dependency & Package Configuration Low 36% bounce and deepbook.tech referrals confirm active DeFi developers needing Move.toml guidance.
/references/cli Transaction Building & PTBs 15% bounce rate shows CLI reference users are deeply engaged, correlating with PTB CLI question volume.
/onchain-finance/tokenized-assets/create-nft Object Model & Dynamic Fields 10% bounce rate on NFT creation aligns with high Object Model question volume from DeFi developers.
/references/fullnode-protocol Full Node & Infrastructure Operations 2.5x pageview-to-visitor ratio confirms node operators browse multiple protocol reference pages per session.
Analysis

Notable Takeaways

'How do I deploy to Sui?' was asked at least 10 distinct times verbatim

10+ identical thread_ids for 'How do I deploy to Sui?' across classified_questions

A single canonical deployment quickstart page, if prominently linked from the homepage and chatbot, could deflect a significant fraction of the 72 package deployment questions.

gRPC page drives the longest reading sessions on the entire site yet bot struggled on 76 of 148 migration questions

217s time-on-page for /grpc/using-grpc; 76 uncertain answers out of 148 Data Access Migration questions

Documentation exists and is read carefully, but gaps in API parity coverage are leaving nearly half of migrating developers without confident answers from the bot.

Ecosystem & General Onboarding has the highest bot uncertainty rate of any theme

43 uncertain out of 65 Ecosystem questions (66% uncertainty rate)

Non-technical and beginner questions flood the bot and expose a lack of structured FAQ or routing to appropriate community resources.

DeepBook cross-site referral traffic is the third-largest non-direct source

116 visitors from deepbook.tech, ranking 3rd among all referral sources

An active external DeFi developer community is arriving specifically for DeepBook docs, making DeepBook dependency and integration content a high-leverage documentation investment.

Kapa

Developer Topics

This week's 694 questions span deployment basics, JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration, Move language patterns, object model, security (Seal, multisig, sponsored transactions), package management, and ecosystem onboarding. Migration to gRPC/GraphQL and Move language questions dominate developer activity, alongside high volumes of general onboarding and ecosystem questions. Questions grouped by docs.sui.io category, then by specific topic.

Q: 使用json-rpc老的方案能不能做到？

Sui Tokenomics and Staking

1 certain — spot check
Q: 怎么挖矿
A: 在 Sui 网络中，没有传统意义上的"挖矿"（PoW 工作量证明）机制。Sui 使用的是**委托权益证明（DPoS）**机制。您可以通过以下两种方式参与并获得收益： --- ## 方式一：质押 SUI 代币（Staking） 任何 SUI 持有者都可以将代币质押给验证者，从而获得质押奖励。 - 质押后，代币在整个 epoch（约 24 小时）内锁定。 - 新的质押请求在下一个 epoch …

Q: If they are in the same checkpoint, is it guaranteed the second transaction is ordered after the first? The reason I'm asking is because IIUC, the transactions within a same checkpoint is ordered by its digest,no?
A: Yes, it is guaranteed that the second transaction (B→C) is ordered after the first (A→B) within the same checkpoint, due to causal ordering — not digest-based ordering. Regarding your assumption abou…

1 question

Full Node State Inconsistency Recovery

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: 并不是落后checkpoint，而是同步后和链上状态不一致，那么我觉得应该是某个接受的checkpoint数据有问题，那么怎么快速恢复呢
A: 你的判断很有道理——如果节点已经同步到最新 checkpoint，但本地状态与链上不一致，很可能是某个 checkpoint 数据在接收或执行时出现了问题，导致本地数据库处于不一致状态。 根据知识库中的信息，**最快的恢复方式仍然是从快照重建**，因为这可以绕过有问题的本地数据库状态，直接恢复到一个经过协议验证的干净状态。 ## 推荐：使用 Formal Snapshot 恢复 Formal…
View thread →

Q: SUI 精度是多少？

Hot Potato Pattern

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: HOT是什么概念？
A: # Hot Potato（热土豆）模式 在 Sui 的 Move 语言中，**Hot Potato** 是指一个**没有任何能力（abilities）的结构体**。具体来说，它没有 `copy`、`drop`、`key` 或 `store` 这四种能力中的任何一种。[[Hot Potato Pattern](https://move-book.com/programmability/hot-po…

1 question

Non-Sui Question (Programming Languages)

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: 为什么我在SUI的区块链浏览器里查看了几十个包，它们的Owner都是Immutable?意味着这些合约不可再进行升级了吗？
A: 这是 Sui 上包（Package）的正常现象，下面解释原因： ## 为什么包的 Owner 显示为 Immutable？ 在 Sui 上，**所有已发布的包（Package）本质上都是不可变（Immutable）对象**。这是 Sui 的设计机制：包一旦发布到链上，其字节码就永远不会改变。[[Upgrading Packages](https://docs.sui.io/develop/pu…
View thread →

Q: 代币转账请求付费的grpc simulateTransaction 接口报错PERMISSION_DENIED: permission denied。 但是使用官方节点https://fullnode.mainnet.sui.io:443可以正常预估出来 请问rpc节点有什么配置错误影响了？
A: ## RPC 节点配置问题分析 您的付费 RPC 节点返回 `PERMISSION_DENIED`，而官方节点正常，问题很可能出在以下 **节点配置层面**： ### 1. gRPC 索引未启用 这是最常见的原因。gRPC `simulateTransaction` 接口需要节点**专门开启 gRPC 索引**才能提供服务。如果您的 RPC 提供商没有在 `fullnode.yaml` 中启…

Sprint Planning

Recommended Actions

Doc Actions
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Publish a side-by-side JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration reference covering all common operations

New migration guide page under /develop/accessing-data with code tabs for every deprecated JSON-RPC method and its gRPC or GraphQL equivalent.

76 of 148 migration questions went unanswered confidently, and the July 2026 deprecation deadline is only 3 months away.

↑ Reduce bot uncertainty rate on the highest-volume theme from 51% and deflect repetitive migration questions.
The docs have separate GraphQL and gRPC sections under /develop/accessing-data but no side-by-side JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL migration reference with code tabs exists at https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/index.md or any sub-page.
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Create a pinned deployment quickstart consolidating CLI setup, Move.toml, gas budget, and package ID capture

Single canonical /getting-started/deploy page linked prominently from the homepage and install page.

The same 'How do I deploy to Sui?' question was asked verbatim at least 10 times, accounting for significant chatbot load.

↑ Deflect a large share of the 72 package deployment questions and reduce onboarding drop-off.
There is a Hello World page and onboarding pages but no single canonical /getting-started/deploy page consolidating CLI setup, Move.toml, gas budget, and package ID capture as described, per the site index at https://docs.sui.io/llms.txt.
medium Documentation Partial

Add an Object Model deep-dive guide with diagrams covering all ownership types, dynamic fields, kiosk, and Display V2

New guide under /develop/objects synthesizing ownership, dynamic fields, TTO, kiosk, and Display V2 in one navigable resource.

68 questions with 50% uncertainty and direct user feedback ('the display docs I'm looking at are bad') confirm current docs are fragmented.

↑ Reduce object model uncertainty and decrease repeated kiosk and Display V2 questions.
Object ownership types, dynamic fields, transfers, display, and TTO each have individual pages under /develop/objects (e.g. https://docs.sui.io/develop/objects/index.md) but no single synthesizing deep-dive guide with diagrams covering all topics including kiosk and Display V2 exists.
Tooling Actions
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Improve sui client error messages for common publish and PTB failures to include actionable remediation hints

Enhance error output in sui client publish and sui client ptb commands to surface fix suggestions for top failure patterns (missing modules, gas underestimation, type argument errors, PowerShell argument truncation).

Multiple questions this week were triggered directly by opaque CLI errors with no remediation hint, including PowerShell argument errors and 'No modules found' publish failures.

↑ Reduce the volume of basic CLI error questions reaching the chatbot and docs by surfacing fixes at point of failure.
The CLI reference at https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/client.md documents commands but there is no mention of enhanced error messages with actionable remediation hints for common publish and PTB failures.
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Add SDK-level validation warnings when SuiGrpcClient is passed to Transaction.build() without a JSON-RPC fallback client

Emit a clear error or warning in the TypeScript SDK when Transaction resolution is attempted with SuiGrpcClient, directing developers to use a SuiClient for resolution.

Multiple developers this week hit the opaque 'Transaction resolution is not supported with the GRPC client' error mid-migration, causing confusion and wasted debugging time.

↑ Eliminate a common migration pitfall and reduce gRPC migration support questions.
No documentation exists describing SDK-level validation warnings when SuiGrpcClient is passed to Transaction.build() without a JSON-RPC fallback, as no such guidance appears in the gRPC or SDK pages listed in the site index.
DX Actions
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Build a new CLI command to inspect and display the full linear upgrade history of a package

New sui client upgrade-history command that queries the chain and outputs original ID, each published-at version, and chain-id for all upgrades.

Developers asked how to check linear upgrade history from the CLI this week and the knowledge base confirmed no such command exists today.

↑ Give package maintainers direct visibility into upgrade lineage, reducing upgrade management errors and support questions.
The CLI reference at https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/client.md lists various sui client commands but no 'upgrade-history' command for inspecting the full linear upgrade history of a package is documented.
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Create a new sui client dry-run-batch command or SDK utility for simulating multiple PTBs in sequence before execution

New CLI flag or SDK method allowing developers to submit multiple transaction blocks for sequential dry-run simulation, returning gas estimates and execution traces for each.

High PTB question volume this week included repeated gas estimation, coin merging limit, and simulation failure questions that a batch dry-run surface would resolve pre-execution.

↑ Reduce PTB construction errors reaching mainnet and decrease the volume of gas and simulation questions in the chatbot.
The PTB CLI reference at https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/ptb.md and SDK docs do not mention a dry-run-batch command or utility for simulating multiple PTBs in sequence before execution.
docs.sui.io Docs Report · 2026-04-20 to 2026-04-26 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260427T155118Z
